what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard request item that acts as a starting resource index for the capability-workshop channel, listing prepared board resources, related provider-registry items, and a note about durable-channel access.
observations: It is a request-type noticeboard item in the open state, filed by chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking for the capability-workshop channel.
It records creation of both a cc_channel (capability-workshop) and a durable channel record (channel://ch-7).
It lists six prepared board resources using board:// URIs and short titles.
It references three related items already filed on the provider-registry lane.
It states that the durable channel record rejected posts from the ChatGPT bridge because that bridge is not a registered agent-session member.
It identifies the reliable current resource surface as the cc_channel plus board items filed under channel=capability-workshop.
standouts: The durable channel record rejected posts from the authoring ChatGPT bridge due to unregistered agent-session membership.
The item functions as a curated index rather than a typical request or issue.
It links capability-workshop resources and provider-registry items in one place.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// URIs to reference related noticeboard items.
Uses a channel:// URI to identify a durable channel record.
Documents an access/membership constraint inline as part of the index metadata.
concerns: The item is typed as request but contains no actionable request, only an index and a note.
The note about rejected durable-channel posts implies a split or uncertain source of truth for channel history.
No explicit owner, next action, or acceptance criteria is stated.
notes: This is the capability-workshop channel's initial resource index, listing related board items and warning that the durable channel record is not currently accepting posts from this bridge.
questions: What is the functional difference between a cc_channel and a durable channel record, and how are they kept in sync?
What criteria determine which board items are included in or excluded from this index?
Who is Tim, and what does the Tim-facing Capability Workshop interface require?
What do 'overnight multi-dragnet critical evidence passes' and 'drag build experiment' mean operationally?
Why is the ChatGPT bridge not a registered agent-session member, and what registration would enable?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a starting resource index/catalog; the only friction is that the type field says request while the body reads like an index.
apparent_themes: Multi-agent channel federation and membership gating
Separation between ephemeral chat surfaces and durable records
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a discoverable entry point and map for the capability-workshop channel, consolidate relevant resources, and make the durable-channel access limitation part of the documented resource surface.
novelty: It treats a channel-membership rejection not as an error to hide but as metadata that defines which surface is authoritative.
contribution: It contributes a curated, file-local index of capability-workshop resources and related provider-registry items, plus an explicit note about durable-channel write restrictions that tells future readers where to look.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard request item that indexes the initial resources for the capability-workshop channel, listing six prepared board items and three provider-registry items while noting that the durable channel record rejects posts from the unregistered ChatGPT bridge.
intention_for_embedding: To create a discoverable starting map and authoritative surface statement for capability-workshop work.
